summary: Phase 2 Flows — Backend 10/11 pytest PASS (1 transient flake on conversation autocreate; passes individually). Frontend: list page, builder shell, palette, canvas (7 nodes for cart_recovery), config tab default + node selection, Analytics KPIs + sparkline + per-node breakdown, AI side panel (Dev chips + ai-thinking + 'Dev updated the flow' toast), publish on draft, status filter, search, kebab Resume action, /flows/builder/new empty-overlay — all PASS. Marquee handoff via Aryan intel-card BLOCKED: LLM consistently classifies Aryan payday prompt as 'creative_preview' artefact (same finding as Phase 1 iteration_2). The from_brief_id endpoint itself works when given a real flow_brief artefact; verified manually with a Dev-pinned 'Build a cart abandonment recovery flow' convo — but that convo also did NOT generate a flow_brief artefact (LLM produced plain agent reply without an artefact attachment). No console errors on /flows or /flows/builder/flow_cart_recovery_01.

minor.issue: LLM orchestrator does NOT reliably attach a flow_brief artefact even when prompted 'Build a cart abandonment recovery flow' with pinned_agent=dev. Replay returned only role=user + role=agent (dev) text reply, no artefact field. Without the artefact, POST /api/flows {from_brief_id} falls back to creating an empty draft (no nodes/edges).

Dev LLM sometimes replies with text only and no flow_modification (no is_modification=true). Tested 'Add a 24h wait after the WhatsApp message' on review_req — modification arrived once and node count went 4→5; on a second UI invocation, the chip click yielded 'updated the flow' toast but node count stayed at 4 (LLM rewrote without adding). Inconsistent across runs.

When no flow_brief artefact exists on the brief conversation, endpoint silently creates an empty draft (nodes=[], edges=[]) + still creates the Completed task. Suggest 400/422 when no artefact found, OR auto-extract from agent text.

ui_bugs.issue: Tab is HTML-disabled (good) but the spec also mentions [data-testid='analytics-disabled'] which is MISSING. Either add the testid placeholder element OR update spec — current disabled state is enforced via Radix `disabled` prop only.

integration_issues.issue: BLOCKED end-to-end. Clicking intel-card-primary-aryan opens conversation-panel and Build Mode panel renders, BUT artefact_type is 'creative_preview' (Zara variants UI) — NOT flow_brief. So there is no [data-testid='flow-approve'] button to click, and the user journey to /flows/builder/<id> can't complete via Aryan's card. Same root cause as Phase 1 iteration_2 (LLM orchestrator picks creative_preview for payday-window prompt). To unblock, either change Aryan's card prompt to be flow-building (e.g. 'Build a payday-window automation flow') OR force pinned_agent='dev' on this CTA OR force artefact_type='flow_brief' for Aryan's primary CTA.

action_items: Unblock the marquee Phase 1→Phase 2 handoff: deterministically produce a flow_brief artefact for Aryan's 'Build this' CTA (or any seed Build CTA). Options: hardcode artefact_type='flow_brief' on the Aryan card CTA, OR ship a deterministic /api/flows/from-intelligence-card endpoint that bypasses the LLM and copies a static brief.

Make /api/flows/{id}/messages always return is_modification=true when the prompt clearly mutates the canvas (e.g., 'add', 'remove', 'insert', 'split' verbs) — currently it sometimes returns text only with no node change.
Reject POST /api/flows {from_brief_id} with 400 when no flow_brief artefact is found on the source conversation (instead of silently creating an empty draft).
Add a [data-testid='analytics-disabled'] placeholder element in the Analytics tab panel when the flow is in draft state, to match the spec.
Consider de-duplicating the Cart Abandonment Recovery 'Untitled flow' leftover that gets created if tests don't reseed — observed in initial KPI count (Total=5 instead of 4).
critical_code_review_comments: routes/flows.py:265 returns the flow doc via find_one AFTER insert_one — works but could just return doc directly to save a roundtrip.

routes/flows.py _get_or_create_flow_conversation: race condition possible if two requests hit /conversation simultaneously — could create two convs for the same flow_id. Add a unique index on conversations.flow_id or wrap in an upsert.
routes/flows.py: there is no validation that nodes[].type matches one of {trigger,wait,channel,condition,end} on PUT/POST. Frontend renderers will silently fall through if LLM emits a new type.

seed.py _seed_flows uses count_documents excluding deleted — good — but admin/reseed first deletes all flows AND conversations are preserved, leaving orphan convs with flow_id pointing at re-created (same id) flows. Works only because ids are stable.

FlowBuilder right-panel: Analytics tab is disabled for draft but the spec asks for a visible 'Not enough data' placeholder via analytics-disabled testid — currently the click is just blocked with no user-facing explainer.

success_rate.backend: 91% (10/11; 1 flake passes in isolation, 1 skip on handoff due to LLM)
success_rate.frontend: ~90% — every Phase 2 builder/list flow works; only the Phase 1→2 handoff via Aryan's card and the analytics-disabled testid fail

seed_data_creation: Tests call POST /api/admin/reseed at session start AND after every state-modifying test. Final state reseeded — flows count = 4, tasks = 10 with 3/3/2/2 distribution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Builder URL is /flows/builder/:id. POST /api/admin/reseed restores 4 flows + 10 tasks. data-testids verified: page-flows, flows-table, flow-row-*, flow-link-*, flow-actions-*, flows-search, flows-status-filter, flows-kpi-{total|active|paused|drafts}, flows-create-btn, flow-builder, builder-topbar, builder-name, builder-status-pill, builder-publish/pause/resume, node-palette, palette-item-*, canvas-empty-overlay, builder-right-panel, right-tab-{config|ai|analytics}, config-flow-settings, config-node-*, ai-input, ai-send, ai-suggested-*, ai-thinking, analytics-{entered|completed|conv|revenue|sparkline|nodes}. The Analytics tab is disabled on draft via HTML `disabled` attr (not aria-disabled). The handoff issue is an LLM classification problem upstream in Phase 1's orchestrator — Aryan's 'payday window' prompt yields creative_preview reliably. Re-test handoff only AFTER main agent forces flow_brief for the Build CTA. LLM calls average 12-20s. After every test that hits /messages or /publish/pause/resume, reseed.
rca of the issue: Phase 1→2 handoff RCA: routes/conversations.py orchestrator delegates artefact_type selection to GPT-5-mini via structured output. Aryan's seeded card text ('Payday window starts in 3 days. Your top buyers segment hasn't received a campaign in 21 days. Est ₹1.8L upside') is semantically a campaign/creative ask, so the model classifies it as creative_preview (correctly, by its own taxonomy). The Phase 2 spec assumed it would be flow_brief. Mitigation: (a) override artefact_type=flow_brief when the user clicks intel-card-primary-aryan (pass a hint param to /api/conversations), OR (b) change Aryan's seed CTA to 'Build payday flow' wording, OR (c) introduce a deterministic POST /api/flows/from-intel-card/{card_id} that hand-crafts the flow_brief without an LLM round-trip. Same root cause applies to the from_brief_id manual test — Dev-pinned 'Build a cart abandonment recovery flow' also did not yield a flow_brief artefact, suggesting the artefact emission path itself is fragile and worth instrumenting.
